raw_text:
DIRECTLY OBSERVED THERAPY (DOT) CENTRE - INITIAL EVALUATION
Date: 2026-01-08
Facility: Rajan Babu Institute for Pulmonary Medicine and Tuberculosis, New Delhi, India

Patient: 35-year-old Indian male referred for management of multi-drug resistant tuberculosis (MDR-TB).

HPI: Patient initially presented 10 months ago with chronic productive cough, low-grade evening fever, night sweats, and 9 kg weight loss over 4 months. He was diagnosed with pulmonary tuberculosis based on sputum smear positivity (2+ AFB) and started on first-line RNTCP Category I regimen (2HRZE/4HR). After 5 months of treatment, he continued to have persistent cough with sputum production. Repeat sputum smear remained positive (1+ AFB). GeneXpert MTB/RIF detected rifampicin resistance. Culture and phenotypic drug susceptibility testing (DST) confirmed resistance to both isoniazid and rifampicin, consistent with MDR-TB. Second-line DST showed susceptibility to fluoroquinolones, bedaquiline, linezolid, and amikacin. He is HIV negative (tested twice). He has no history of diabetes mellitus. He works as an auto-rickshaw driver and lives in a one-room dwelling with his wife and two children (ages 4 and 7). He is a non-smoker and denies alcohol use.

Vitals: BP 110/70 mmHg, HR 88 bpm, Temp 37.6C, RR 20, SpO2 95% RA. Weight 52 kg (from baseline 61 kg), Height 170 cm, BMI 18.0.

Physical Exam: Thin, chronically ill-appearing male. No peripheral lymphadenopathy. Respiratory: decreased breath sounds right upper zone with coarse crackles. No wheeze. Heart normal. Abdomen soft, no hepatosplenomegaly. No clubbing.

Labs: Sputum smear: 1+ AFB. GeneXpert: MTB detected, rifampicin resistance detected. Culture: M. tuberculosis, resistant to isoniazid (MIC >1 mcg/mL) and rifampicin (MIC >1 mcg/mL). Susceptible to levofloxacin, moxifloxacin, bedaquiline, linezolid, clofazimine, amikacin. HIV 1/2 antibody: Negative. HbA1c 5.4%. Hepatitis B and C: negative. CBC: Hgb 11.2, WBC 8.4, Plt 310. LFTs: AST 28, ALT 32, bilirubin 0.6. Creatinine 0.8. Baseline audiometry: normal. Baseline ECG: QTc 410 ms (normal). Visual acuity: 6/6 bilaterally.

Chest X-ray: Right upper lobe cavity (3.5 cm) with surrounding infiltrates. Left lung clear.

Assessment and Plan:
1. Multi-drug resistant pulmonary tuberculosis (MDR-TB), sputum smear positive. HIV negative. Initiate all-oral shorter MDR-TB regimen per India NTEP 2025 guidelines: bedaquiline 400mg daily x2 weeks then 200mg 3x/week, levofloxacin 750mg daily, linezolid 600mg daily, and clofazimine 100mg daily for 9 months.
2. Monthly sputum smear and culture for conversion monitoring. Target: sputum culture conversion by month 4.
3. ECG monitoring every 2 weeks for QTc prolongation (bedaquiline + levofloxacin). Hold medications if QTc >500ms.
4. Monthly CBC for linezolid-related myelosuppression. Visual acuity checks monthly (linezolid optic neuropathy).
5. LFTs monthly. Baseline and monthly audiometry.
6. Nutritional supplementation: high-protein diet counseling, pyridoxine 100mg daily.
7. Household contact screening: wife and children require clinical evaluation, TST/IGRA, and chest X-ray. Refer for TB preventive therapy if indicated.
8. DOT supervision confirmed. Social support referral for nutritional and financial assistance.
9. Follow-up weekly for first month, then monthly.
medications: Prior: Failed first-line RNTCP Category I regimen (2HRZE/4HR)
